title: Towards multimodal foundation models in molecular cell biology

data_scope.article_type: Perspective/Review - no new primary data generated
data_scope.reference_atlas_hca: Millions of human cells across organs (ongoing)
data_scope.reference_model_geneformer: ~30 million single-cell transcriptomes (cited from Theodoris et al. Nature 2023)
data_scope.data_availability: No new data released with this Perspective

core_conclusion: Multimodal foundation models pretrained on diverse omics datasets (genomics, transcriptomics, epigenomics, proteomics, metabolomics, spatial profiling) can produce holistic cell/gene/tissue maps and, via context-specific transfer learning, enable unprecedented applications from cell-type recognition and biomarker discovery to in silico perturbation, defining a new AI-empowered paradigm for molecular cell biology.
why_it_matters: Provides the first coherent architecture roadmap for unifying siloed omics AI models; frames the data-to-insight bottleneck and positions multimodal FMs as the scalable solution; catalogues concrete biomedical applications and a prioritized research agenda including lab-in-the-loop active learning.
why_nature: Paradigm-setting cross-disciplinary Perspective with high citation potential across biology, AI, and medicine; extends a Nature-published lineage (AlphaFold, Geneformer, scGPT); addresses responsible AI and patient-data ethics matching Nature's editorial priorities.
added_value_to_my_research: Multimodal pretraining blueprint -> geospatial FM jointly pretrained on imagery, mobility, POIs, census data for health-geography transfer learning
Lab-in-the-loop active learning -> field-survey-in-the-loop directing spatial data collection to highest-uncertainty census tracts for 2SFCA/R2SFCA
In silico perturbation -> counterfactual policy simulation for urban health interventions (clinic siting, transit closure, EV charger placement)
Cross-modal representation learning -> shared place embeddings from heterogeneous urban health data layers replacing hand-engineered feature stacks
Spatial multi-omics integration at scale -> 'urban cell atlas' of fine-grained spatiotemporal records as a geospatial foundation model for spatial health prediction

related.title: Multimodal cell maps as a foundation for structural and functional genomics
related.relation: companion multimodal cell-map paper enabling structural/functional genomics integration

related.title: Decoding omics via representation learning
related.relation: benchmarks autoencoder architectures for omics representation learning (AUTOENCODIX)

related.title: Integrative deep learning of spatial multi-omics with SWITCH

related.title: Scaling and quantization of a foundational deep learning model for network biology
related.relation: demonstrates scaling laws for Geneformer (>100M cells), providing empirical grounding for the scaling vision in this paper
